leads.opportunity: You've already proven the pattern works on /store and /location — bringing the homepage title in line with them is a one-line, same-day fix on your single highest-traffic page. leads.issues: Every page on sallieplumleystudio.com shares the exact same ~75-character title suffix — "Sallie Plumley Studio- Custom Woodworker and Furniture Maker in Richmond, Virginia" — appended after the page name on Shop, Custom Woodwork, Map Shop, Clock Shop, Woodburning/Pyrography, and About Me. That shared block also carries a small but very visible typo baked into the template: "Studio-" is missing the space before the dash, so it's live on every single indexed page at once. Because the suffix is identical everywhere, pages like "Map Shop" or "Clock Shop" only have a handful of unique words left to actually compete on for their own specific searches.
leads.opportunity: Fixing the missing space and shortening the shared suffix is a one-time edit to the site's title template that corrects every page at once. From there, giving each product category (maps, clocks, custom furniture, woodburning) its own distinct, keyword-specific title — and working the studio's urban/salvaged-wood sourcing story (she's listed in the Virginia Urban Wood Business Directory) into the copy — is a same-week job that turns five near-duplicate pages into five pages that can each rank on their own.
